Article Title: Shedeur Sanders Browns Future: "Something to work with": Shedeur Sanders under pressure as Cleveland Browns Head Coach Todd Monken flags key concern
Article Text:
Shedeur Sanders under pressure as Cleveland Browns Head Coach Todd Monken flags key concern(Image via Getty)

Cleveland Browns coach Todd Monken makes an honest point on Shedeur Sanders’ mobility gap in the modern NFL



The Cleveland Browns are heading into a crucial quarterback battle, and all eyes are on Shedeur Sanders. As the young QB enters his second NFL season, expectations are rising. However, on April 16, 2026, head coach Todd Monken made it clear that one part of Sanders’ game still needs work.The concern is simple. In today’s NFL, mobility is no longer optional. It is essential.Speaking to ESPN’s Kevin Clark on April 16, Browns HC Todd Monken did not hold back while evaluating Shedeur Sanders. He explained how the definition of a “mobile quarterback” has changed over time.He said, "I think you see playmaking ability. I think you see a player that is confident in his skillset. So when you see that playmaking ability and inner confidence, you say, 'Okay, there's something to work with there."Head Coach further added, “I said this morning that 15 or 20 years ago, they would have said that Shedeur Sanders was athletic,” Monken said. “They would have said he could escape. Well, the game’s changed so much to now he’s not a mobile quarterback. No, he’s mobile. He’s not as mobile as what we’ve come to where Lamar Jackson or the quickness that these players have. Their escape ability is completely changed from when it was.”That comparison says a lot. Players like Lamar Jackson and Josh Allen have redefined the position.They extend plays with ease and turn broken situations into big gains. Sanders, on the other hand, operates differently. He can move, but he needs space and time to build momentum.His numbers support that view. In Colorado in 2024, Sanders threw for 4,134 yards and 37 touchdowns while completing 74 percent of his passes. The efficiency stands out. Even so, he only scored four rushing touchdowns, so his rushing production stayed low. That pattern continued into his first NFL season in 2025, when he threw for 1,400 yards, seven touchdowns, and 10 interceptions, and ran for only a little.Still, this is not a closed case. Monken also pointed to Sanders’ confidence and playmaking ability as strong positives. More importantly, he shifted some responsibility onto the coaching staff. “Now, it’s going to be our fault if we can’t develop them,” Monken added in the same interaction.That puts pressure on both sides. Sanders must adapt to the league's pace, while the Browns must build a system that fits his strengths. With Deshaun Watson and Dillon Gabriel also in the mix, the competition remains wide open. Mobility alone will not decide the starter. But in today’s NFL, it might just decide how far a quarterback can go.
Article Summary: Cleveland Browns coach Todd Monken makes an honest point on Shedeur Sanders’ mobility gap in the modern NFL. Monken: “I said this morning that 15 or 20 years ago, they would have said that ShedeurSanders was athletic. Well, the game’s changed so much to now he's not a mobile quarterback. No, he's mobile"

Article Title: Nfl Trade Rumors: NFL Trade Rumors: Los Angeles Rams and San Francisco 49ers linked to top offensive tackle prospect ahead of 2026 draft
Article Text:
NFL Trade Rumors: Los Angeles Rams and San Francisco 49ers linked to top offensive tackle prospect ahead of 2026 draft (Getty)

NFL trade rumors: Los Angeles Rams and San Francisco 49ers circle Caleb Lomu as 2026 NFL draft plans hint at possible moves

The latest NFL trade rumors are starting to circle around Utah offensive tackle Caleb Lomu as the 2026 NFL Draft gets closer. The Los Angeles Rams and the San Francisco 49ers have both spent time evaluating Lomu in recent days. That growing interest is starting to stand out across the league.With just days left before the draft, these interactions carry more weight. Teams often stay quiet this time of year, but actions speak louder. And right now, both NFC contenders seem to be tracking Caleb Lomu closely as part of their draft plans.The Los Angeles Rams recently spent time with Caleb Lomu, according to Ian Rapoport of NFL Network, who shared the update during a draft report earlier this week. The exact nature of the meeting remains unclear, but the interest itself is telling.The Rams are stable at tackle for now. Alaric Jackson and Warren McClendon Jr. are expected to start. Still, McClendon is set to hit free agency after the season. That makes Caleb Lomu a smart long-term option. He can add depth immediately while giving the team leverage in future contract talks.There is also a draft-day angle here. Caleb Lomu is widely projected to go in the later part of the first round.That fits a possible trade-back scenario for Los Angeles. The Rams could move down, collect extra picks, and still land their target.At the same time, the San Francisco 49ers are doing similar groundwork. On April 15, Ian Rapoport reported that the 49ers are also meeting Caleb Lomu after hosting Alabama tackle Kadyn Proctor earlier in the process. That back-to-back evaluation at the same position raises questions.San Francisco holds the No. 27 pick. If Caleb Lomu is high on their board, they may not want to risk waiting. His draft range has been fluid, with several teams in the 20s showing interest. That creates a real chance of a trade-up if the 49ers want control.There is also the Trent Williams factor. Recent contract tensions have brought some focus to the future of the position. Even if Williams remains the starter, adding Caleb Lomu now could be a forward-looking move.At this point, teams are unlikely to reveal their complete strategy. But with both the Los Angeles Rams and San Francisco 49ers tracking Caleb Lomu this closely, his name is gaining serious traction in trade rumors heading into draft night.
Article Summary: The Los Angeles Rams and the San Francisco 49ers have both spent time evaluating Caleb Lomu in recent days. The Utah offensive tackle is widely projected to go in the later part of the first round. The Rams are stable at tackle for now, but Warren McClendon Jr. is set to hit free agency.

Article Title: Britt McHenry drops cryptic 'X' post gloating over Dianna Russini's epic downfall amid fistfight rumors
Article Text:
Britt McHenry and Dianna Russini (Getty Images)

What exactly is driving the Dianna Russini controversy



The fallout around Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel has quickly moved beyond a few photos and into a wider debate about perception, access, and credibility in sports media. Images published by Page Six showed the two spending time together at a hotel, setting off a wave of speculation. Both are married, which only intensified the reaction online. Within hours, assumptions hardened into narratives. Russini now faces an internal review at The Athletic, even as she and Vrabel firmly reject any suggestion of wrongdoing.At the center of this is not just what the photos show, but what they don’t. Russini addressed that gap directly, saying, “The photos don’t represent the group of six people who were hanging out during the day.” It’s a detail that complicates the story, though it hasn’t slowed the noise around it. In an era where partial glimpses often shape full opinions, context tends to arrive late, if at all.Vrabel’s response was even more blunt. “These photos show a completely innocent interaction and any suggestion otherwise is laughable,” he said. “This doesn’t deserve any further response.” His words reflect a clear attempt to shut the conversation down, but the story has already taken on a life of its own.What has added another layer is the resurfacing of an old allegation involving Russini and Britt McHenry. The story, which dates back to their time covering Washington, describes a reported altercation tied to overlapping personal relationships. It remains unverified in many respects, yet its timing has amplified scrutiny on Russini’s past and present.McHenry has not stayed silent. Posting on X, she wrote, “Me the last week on X,” alongside a karma-themed GIF linked to Taylor Swift . The message was brief but pointed, hinting at a long-standing tension that is now spilling into public view.Beyond the headlines, there is a more uncomfortable question circulating quietly. Russini built her reputation on access and well-sourced reporting, often breaking stories others couldn’t. Now, some are unfairly tying that success to speculation about personal relationships. It’s a line of thinking that says more about the industry’s biases than it does about evidence.For now, the facts remain limited, the responses clear, and the consequences still unfolding.
Article Summary: Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel are married, which complicates the story. Russini built her reputation on access and well-sourced reporting. Some are unfairly tying that success to speculation about personal relationships. It's a line of thinking that says more about industry's biases than it does about evidence.

Article Title: Fact check: Did Justin Herbert and Charlie Kirk share lunch outing in Los Angeles
Article Text:
Justin Herbert (Getty Images)

What sparked the Justin Herbert lunch rumor , and why is it incorrect



A routine lunch outing has unexpectedly pulled Justin Herbert into an online swirl, though not for anything tied to football. The Los Angeles Chargers quarterback was photographed at a restaurant in Los Angeles alongside his girlfriend, Madison Beer, whose recent beach photos have already kept her trending. But it was a man seated across from Herbert who caught social media’s attention, sparking a wave of speculation that quickly drifted far from reality.The image, widely shared on X, led some users to wonder if Justin Herbert was dining with Charlie Kirk . The guesses came quickly and with confidence. “Is that who I think it is???” one user asked, zooming into the photo. Another posted a separate image of Kirk for comparison, while a third joked, “Charlie Scheffler? Scotty Kirk?”The problem is simple. Kirk cannot be the person in that photo. He was killed on September 10, 2025, during a speaking event at Utah Valley University. Authorities charged Tyler Robinson with aggravated murder in the case. That alone puts the speculation to rest, though the speed at which the rumor spread says plenty about how quickly online narratives can form around incomplete information.Herbert, for his part, has far more pressing concerns than internet guesswork. The 2025 season tested him in ways that rarely show up in highlight reels.A battered offensive line limited what the Chargers could do offensively, leaving their franchise quarterback under constant pressure. Despite that, the team still managed an 11-6 record, only to exit in the first round of the playoffs once again.Injuries played a major role. Rookie Joe Alt featured in just six games, while Rashawn Slater missed the entire season. That instability up front forced adjustments week after week, often at the cost of rhythm and protection.There is a clear shift in approach heading into 2026. The Chargers have both draft capital and cap flexibility, tools they are expected to use to rebuild the line in front of Herbert. Holding the 22nd overall pick, along with selections at 55, 86, 123, and 204, the front office has room to be aggressive if needed.For now, what began as a casual lunch photo has turned into a brief internet mystery. The reality is far less dramatic, and far more grounded in a team trying to give its quarterback a fair chance to succeed.
Article Summary: The Los Angeles Chargers quarterback was photographed at a restaurant in Los Angeles. A man seated across from Herbert sparked a wave of speculation that quickly drifted far from reality. The reality is far less dramatic, and far more grounded in a team trying to give its quarterback a fair chance to succeed.

Article Title: Crissy Froyd fired from USA Today after controversial comments targeting Dianna Russini over Mike Vrabel photos
Article Text:
Crissy Froyd fired from USA Today after criticizing Dianna Russini for Mike Vrabel photos (Getty Images)

Why was Crissy Froyd fired after her comments on Dianna Russini



What did Crissy Froyd say and why does she stand by it



NFL reporter Crissy Froyd lost her role at USA Today Sports, after she publicly targeted fellow journalist Dianna Russini over a controversy tied to Mike Vrabel. The decision came swiftly on Thursday, with the outlet citing professionalism concerns. What began as commentary on a rival reporter’s exit quickly escalated into a broader debate about ethics, credibility, and how far personal opinion can stretch in a public-facing role.The trigger was not subtle. Crissy Froyd’s remarks about Dianna Russini, posted on X, gained traction fast and pulled her employer into the same spotlight. USA Today moved quickly to distance itself.“USA TODAY Sports has ended its contractor relationship with Crissy Froyd effective immediately. Her recent statements do not reflect our commitment to professionalism or uphold our principles of ethical conduct,” the outlet said in a statement on Thursday.Behind the scenes, the concern had already been flagged. Froyd revealed she received an internal email pointing to the growing fallout. The message warned that her posts were becoming a “news-making cycle” and directly linking her views to the company. It also stressed that much of the discussion around Russini remained unverified, something the publication would not carry on its own platform.That distinction appears to have mattered. Froyd was not writing for the outlet when she posted, but the association proved difficult to separate once her name and employer were tied together in coverage.Froyd has not backed down. If anything, her response has been firmer since the dismissal. “I want to say firstly that I do not regret anything that I said and that I stand behind the fact it is all indeed true.”She acknowledged the personal cost but framed it as a line she was willing to cross. “I want to thank USA TODAY SMG for the incredible run I had there for about half of my life. It is deeply, deeply emotional to me that this relationship has come to an end and that my contract has been terminated because of this.”Her criticism of Russini had been sharp and public, questioning both credibility and professional conduct. She went further in subsequent remarks, calling the situation “incredibly interesting” in how it was handled across the industry, and raising concerns about double standards.“I would never say anything I didn’t stand behind because I know the potential consequences, even if a situation seems risky,” she said. “Thank you to everyone who has supported me both before and after I released my statements.”Froyd also leaned into a broader message about accountability. “Regardless of what happens to me in the future, I want to say something. Do not be afraid to potentially martyr yourself for the right causes.”Meanwhile, Russini has denied any wrongdoing tied to the photos, while Vrabel dismissed the speculation outright, calling it “a completely innocent interaction.”The episode leaves a familiar tension hanging in the air. Personal platforms offer reach and freedom, but the line between individual voice and professional responsibility remains thin, and increasingly fragile.
Article Summary: NFL reporter Crissy Froyd lost her role at USA Today Sports. She publicly targeted fellow journalist Dianna Russini over a controversy tied to Mike Vrabel. The decision came swiftly on Thursday, with the outlet citing professionalism concerns.

Article Title: Dak Prescott’s relationship with ex Sarah Jane Ramos takes unexpected turn with family agreement before court case
Article Text:
dak-prescott-sarah-ramos (Getty Images)

Why did Dak Prescott and Sarah Jane Ramos settle custody before court



What led to Dak Prescott and Sarah Jane Ramos’ breakup and legal action



The offseason spotlight around Dak Prescott has shifted from football to family matters, but a potential courtroom clash has been avoided. Prescott and his former fiancée Sarah Jane Ramos reached a temporary custody agreement for their two daughters just days before a scheduled hearing in Collin County, Texas. The judge signed off on the arrangement, calling it in the children’s best interest. It brings a measure of calm to what has been a tense and very public stretch following the sudden cancellation of their wedding.The timing says a lot. With a hearing looming, both sides chose to resolve matters privately rather than extend a dispute into open court. The agreement remains informal and will only be filed if enforcement becomes necessary, a detail that suggests both parties are trying to keep control over sensitive family decisions.Ramos’ attorney, Raymond Rafool, made the tone clear. “Sarah Jane is very happy that she and Dak are able to make decisions for the parties’ girls and their family on their own,” he said. “She firmly believes that working together as parents is very important and will have long-term benefits for the girls. Sarah Jane continues to be dedicated to putting the girls’ best interests and lives first.”Prescott’s legal team echoed that approach. “This agreement is a positive step forward and reflects the commitment Dak has to raising their daughters in a healthy, supportive environment,” attorney Levi G.McCathern II said. “We’re pleased this was resolved amicably and outside of the courtroom. At the end of the day, the focus remains where it should be — on the well-being of Dak’s two daughters — and he is fully committed to ensuring that. ”The custody discussion did not emerge in isolation. It followed a rapid breakdown in their relationship. Prescott filed a Suit Affecting the Parent-Child Relationship in March, seeking joint managing conservatorship of their daughters, Margaret and Aurora. The filing is common in Texas for unmarried parents looking to define rights and responsibilities.Just weeks earlier, the couple had called off their April wedding in Lake Como. An email sent to guests captured the tone: “As many of you know, our wedding is currently scheduled to take place in Lake Como, Italy, on Friday April 10, 2026. It is with heavy hearts that we privately inform our attendees that we have made the very difficult decision to cancel our wedding. We deeply apologize for any inconvenience this may cause you and we welcome your prayers. Sarah Jane Ramos and Dak Prescott.”Reports around the split pointed to trust issues, though Prescott’s camp pushed back, urging privacy. “The decision to call off the wedding was mutual,” his attorney said earlier. “Unfortunately, when well-known people go through something personal, speculation tends to run wild, and the truth is often swept to the side. People should stop inventing stories and respect their privacy.”For now, the legal temperature has cooled. The larger questions about their relationship remain, but both sides appear aligned on one point that matters most.
Article Summary: Dak Prescott and his former fiancée Sarah Jane Ramos reached a temporary custody agreement. The judge signed off on the arrangement, calling it in the children’s best interest. The agreement remains informal and will only be filed if enforcement becomes necessary.

Article Title: NFL Trade Rumors: Carolina Panthers linked to blockbuster deal with $72M New York Giants' star player
Article Text:
The Carolina Panthers are emerging as a potential trade partner for the New York Giants amid growing buzz around a defensive upgrade. Analysts believe such a move could significantly strengthen Carolina’s front line and ease pressure on the rest of the defense. However, any deal would likely require a hefty price as the Panthers push to accelerate their playoff ambitions.

Dexter Lawrence buzz grows as Panthers eye defensive leap



The Carolina Panthers are inching toward relevance again, but the gap between promise and real contention still feels wide. While optimism surrounds Bryce Young and a reshaped roster, the defense remains one bold move away from turning heads across the league. That missing piece could come in the form of a dominant interior force, something the franchise has lacked in defining moments.Across the league, elite teams like the Seattle Seahawks and the Philadelphia Eagles have built their identity in the trenches. Carolina knows that blueprint well. And now, a familiar name has resurfaced in trade whispers, one that could instantly change the Panthers’ ceiling if the front office is willing to pay the price.The speculation centers on Dexter Lawrence, the New York Giants star who has quietly become one of the most disruptive interior defenders in football. With uncertainty brewing around his long-term situation in New York, analysts have begun linking him to potential landing spots and Carolina keeps showing up on that list.As highlighted by James Dator, the fit is not just logical, it is potentially transformative:"Bobby Brown III is the weakest part of the Panthers defense now, and Dexter Lawrence could make Carolina absolutely terrifying.A line with Derrick Brown and Lawrence, set off by Jaelan Phillips rushing off the edge could make for one of the better units in the NFL.The rest of the defensive line would leave something to be desired. But pairing Lawrence with Derrick Brown would instantly give Carolina the most lethal interior rotation in football.Expert NFL picks: Exclusive betting insights only at USA TODAY.That would take a lot of pressure off the second level, which should be much improved following the addition of inside linebacker Devin Lloyd. In fact, it's difficult to imagine a more impactful acquisition for this defense."It is hard to argue with that vision. Pairing Lawrence with Derrick Brown would give Carolina a rare blend of power and consistency inside, while edge presence from Jaelan Phillips adds another layer of chaos for opposing quarterbacks.Still, a move like this would not come cheap. The Carolina Panthers would likely need to part with premium draft capital or key assets. But if the goal is to accelerate the Bryce Young era and push toward meaningful January football, this is the kind of aggressive swing contenders make.
Article Summary: The Carolina Panthers are emerging as a potential trade partner for the New York Giants amid growing buzz around a defensive upgrade. Analysts believe such a move could significantly strengthen Carolina’s front line and ease pressure on the rest of the defense. Any deal would likely require a hefty price as the Panthers push to accelerate their playoff ambitions.

Article Title: Travis Kelce’s NFL future in doubt as Kansas City Chiefs quietly prepare his replacement as his wedding nears
Article Text:
The Kansas City Chiefs are already planning for life after Travis Kelce despite his 2026 return. The team has shown interest in Oregon tight end Kenyon Sadiq as a potential successor. With contract details hinting at a possible exit after 2026, Kansas City appears focused on building a smooth transition for the future.

Chiefs eye future tight end solution as Kelce era nears turning point

The future may still belong to Travis Kelce , but the Kansas City Chiefs are already thinking a step ahead. Even after the veteran tight end confirmed he will return for the 2026 season, subtle signals from the franchise suggest long-term planning is quietly underway. For a team built on sustained dominance, standing still has never been part of the blueprint.That mindset is now shaping their draft outlook. While Kelce remains a central piece of the offense led by Patrick Mahomes , the front office appears keen on preparing for the inevitable transition. And one name has started to surface more often than others.Oregon standout Kenyon Sadiq has quickly emerged as a serious option for Kansas City Chiefs. The team itself added fuel to the conversation by highlighting his college production, noting, “This prospect led all FBS tight ends with eight touchdown grabs last season.” That public endorsement did not go unnoticed.League insiders believe the Chiefs are weighing multiple needs with their top draft pick, but Sadiq remains firmly in the mix. “I think at No. 9, you’re looking at corner, receiver and edge as possibilities, with the need to backstop the tackle position at some point in the draft,” Albert Breer wrote.“Also, the idea of taking Oregon’s Kenyon Sadiq at the very least is a consideration.”The appeal is obvious. Sadiq offers immediate upside while also fitting into a long-term vision. Analysts see him as more than just depth. He could grow into a central figure in the offense. “Even the short-term potential of a Patrick Mahomes-led offense featuring Kelce, Sadiq, [Kenneth] Walker and Xavier Worthy is something I’d buy a ticket to see,” Nick Baumgardner said.“Sadiq’s long-term potential as Kelce’s successor and Mahomes’ new best friend is obvious. He is an extremely special athlete and the prototype for a modern tight end prospect — he can play inline and hold his own against tackles and edges; he’s an excellent space blocker who can deliver a blow on the move; he’s big enough to body small defensive backs and fast enough to run around many of them.”There is also a financial layer to this strategy. “The Chiefs have a long-standing policy of not doing void years, and the above two rules allow them to sidestep that (using a tactic the Eagles have over the years),” Breer wrote.“Kelce has minimum salaries built into dummy years in 2027 and 2028, with a $40 million guarantee for 2028 vesting June 8 of next year—which will force the Chiefs to release him by then, and allow for them to spread the dead money out, since the date falls after June 1.”For now, Travis Kelce remains the heartbeat of Kansas City’s offense. But the groundwork for life after him is no longer a distant thought. It is already in motion.
Article Summary: Travis Kelce confirmed he will return for the 2026 season. But the Kansas City Chiefs are already planning for life after him. The team has shown interest in Oregon tight end Kenyon Sadiq as a potential successor. With contract details hinting at a possible exit after 2026, Kansas City appears focused on a smooth transition.

Article Title: "None of it was even real": Stefon Diggs allegedly calls out Cardi B after shocking DNA test twist
Article Text:
A viral post falsely claimed Stefon Diggs called out Cardi B over a DNA test, even dragging Offset into the narrative. The story has been debunked, with no credible evidence or statements supporting it. An emotional quote fueled the rumor, but it was misattributed, highlighting how quickly misinformation can spread online.

Stefon Diggs Cardi B rumor debunked as false claim spreads online

Rumors move fast online. Facts usually take longer to catch up. That pattern played out again this week when a viral post dragged Stefon Diggs and Cardi B into a messy, emotional story that simply is not true. The claim spread quickly, pulled in thousands of reactions, and left many people convinced something serious had happened.The post suggested Stefon Diggs had called out Cardi B after a DNA test allegedly showed a child was not his. It even tied Offset into the narrative, making it feel more believable to some readers. But there is no real proof behind any of it. No confirmed reports. No statements. No reliable sources. The entire story falls apart under basic fact-checking.What really pushed the rumor into overdrive was a quote from Stefon Diggs that felt deeply personal. It read: “Imagine loving a child with everything in you, waking up every day for them, sacrificing for them... then finding out none of it was even real. I feel broken, disrespected, and completely fooled. I don’t even know what comes next after something like this.”It sounds real. It sounds painful. And that is exactly why people believed it.But there is no evidence that Diggs ever said or posted those words. The quote appears to have been taken from elsewhere online and placed into a completely different context.Once that happened, the internet did what it does best. It filled in the gaps with assumptions.Some users rushed to judge. Others debated timelines and relationships. A few questioned why no one involved had spoken out. But silence does not mean something is true. In many cases, it means the story is not worth addressing.This is how misinformation works. A strong emotion, a familiar name, and a believable setup. That is often enough.At the end of the day, nothing in this viral claim holds up. There is no confirmed DNA test. No public fallout. No verified connection between the people mentioned and the story being told.It is a reminder to slow down. Not everything that trends deserves to be trusted.
Article Summary: A viral post suggested Stefon Diggs had called out Cardi B after a DNA test allegedly showed a child was not his. The story has been debunked, with no credible evidence or statements supporting it. An emotional quote fueled the rumor, but it was misattributed.

Article Title: NFL Trade Rumors: New England Patriots reportedly "frontrunners" to acquire the $96M Philadelphia Eagles star
Article Text:
New England Patriots reportedly "frontrunners" to acquire the $96M Philadelphia Eagles star.(image via X)

New England Patriots linked to the Philadelphia Eagles ’ A.J. Brown after a disturbing season



The New England Patriots have dominated the field in the last season after a terrible season in 2024.Though the team faced a heartbreaking loss in Super Bowl LX against the Seattle Seahawks, the team seems focused on fixing the roster, ahead of the new season.A few hours ago, CBS Sports’ popular NFL insider, Jonathan Jones, hinted that the New England Patriots might be the ones to acquire the Philadelphia Eagles’ star player, A.J. Brown.CBS Sports’ Jonathan Jones noted in a recent report, “For months we have been reporting it is likely A.J. Brown will not be with the Philadelphia Eagles in the 2026 season…The New England Patriots remain the frontrunner to acquire the All-Pro receiver, and what has been true all offseason continues to be true today as it relates to his contract.”CBS Sports’ Jonathan Jones added, “It hurts the Eagles’ 2026 salary cap to deal Brown before June 1, adding an extra $20 million to their cap before that date.”CBS Sports’ Jonathan Jones continued, “Philadelphia has positioned itself to move forward in 2026 with a receivers group that can live without Brown. At this point it would be a surprise if Brown is not on a new team by the start of training camp.”However, the New England Patriots have not confirmed the speculations concerning A.J.Brown yet as the team remains silent.The Philadelphia Eagles’ star player, A.J. Brown went through a lot of interesting events in the last season as his performance recorded a slight decline since 2024.In 2024, A.J. Brown finished with 67 receptions and 1,079 receiving yards across 13 games. In the last season, the star player finished with 78 receptions and 1,003 receiving yards in 15 games.While A.J. Brown dealt with a brutal hamstring injury in the last season, he also made a few controversial comments about the team’s offense.In November, while speaking to the media, A.J. Brown said, "It's not just solely about my situation. I want to win, too…we can't just keep slapping a Band-Aid over the defense doing their job and getting us out of trouble. At what point are we going to pick up our slack as an offense?The popular wide receiver added, "That's what I'm getting at. It's not about, 'I don't care about winning, all I care about is stats.' No. It's been week after week sometimes we're not doing our job on offense…”For now, all eyes are on whether A.J. Brown chooses to stay in the Philadelphia Eagles for the new season.

The New England Patriots have dominated the field in the last season after a terrible season in 2024. The Philadelphia Eagles lost in the Super Bowl LX against the Seattle Seahawks. The team seems focused on fixing the roster ahead of the new season.
